<tool-use data-tool-type="generic" data-tool-name="multi_replace_string_in_file"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **multi_replace_string_in_file**</summary>

**Input:**

- explanation: `Add Bug3 prefix (tread UV on client) and Bug4 (Roam regression) patch registrations + restore MinableSearchArea to 32`
- replacements:

```
[map[filePath:c:\Dev\StationpediaAscendedWorkspace\AimeeBugFixes\Plugin.cs newString:            TryPatch(ref success, ref fail, "Bug3-PhysicsUpdate",
                AccessTools.Method(typeof(WheeledBase), "PhysicsUpdate"),
                postfix: new HarmonyMethod(typeof(Bug3_WheelAnimationSyncFix), nameof(Bug3_WheelAnimationSyncFix.PhysicsUpdate_Postfix)));

            TryPatch(ref success, ref fail, "Bug3-TreadUV",
                AccessTools.Method(typeof(WheeledBase), "PhysicsUpdate"),
                prefix: new HarmonyMethod(typeof(Bug3_WheelAnimationSyncFix), nameof(Bug3_WheelAnimationSyncFix.PhysicsUpdate_Prefix)));

            TryPatch(ref success, ref fail, "Bug4-Roam",
                AccessTools.Method(typeof(RobotMining), "Roam"),
                postfix: new HarmonyMethod(typeof(Bug4_RoamNoOreFix), nameof(Bug4_RoamNoOreFix.Roam_Postfix)));

            // Restore original ore scan radius (was 32, halved to 16 in Orbital Update)
            try
            {
                var searchAreaField = AccessTools.Field(typeof(RobotMining), "MinableSearchArea");
                if (searchAreaField != null)
                {
                    searchAreaField.SetValue(null, 32);
                    Log.LogInfo("  MinableSearchArea restored to 32 (was 16)");
                }
            }
            catch (Exception ex)
            {
                Log.LogWarning($"  Could not restore MinableSearchArea: {ex.Message}");
            }

            Log.LogInfo($"AIMeE Bug Fixes: {success} patches applied, {fail} failed"); oldString:            TryPatch(ref success, ref fail, "Bug3-PhysicsUpdate",
                AccessTools.Method(typeof(WheeledBase), "PhysicsUpdate"),
                postfix: new HarmonyMethod(typeof(Bug3_WheelAnimationSyncFix), nameof(Bug3_WheelAnimationSyncFix.PhysicsUpdate_Postfix)));

            Log.LogInfo($"AIMeE Bug Fixes: {success} patches applied, {fail} failed");]]
```


</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (copilot/claude-opus-4.6)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="generic" data-tool-name="replace_string_in_file"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **replace_string_in_file**</summary>

**Input:**

- filePath: `c:\Dev\StationpediaAscendedWorkspace\AimeeBugFixes\Plugin.cs`
- newString:

```
    // =========================================================================
    // BUG 3: Tread/wheel UV animation broken on multiplayer clients
    //
    // Root cause (revised after server testing):
    //   Server has IsOccluded=False, so AnimateAuthority() already runs.
    //   WheelTransform rotation IS synced via RoverUpdate to clients.
    //   BUT the tread UV scroll animation depends on WheelRpm, which is:
    //     - Set by WheelAudio() from WheelCollider.rpm
    //     - WheelColliders are DISABLED on non-authority clients
    //       (EnableWheelColliders(HasAuthority) in PhysicsUpdate)
    //     - Disabled WheelCollider → GetGroundHit()=false → WheelAudio
    //       early-returns without updating WheelRpm
    //     - Animate() reads WheelRpm=0 → no UV scroll → frozen treads
    //
    // Fix has two parts:
    //   A) PREFIX (client-side): Estimate WheelRpm from synced velocity
    //      before Animate() reads it. Velocity IS synced via
    //      PhysicsUpdateMessage.Velocity → RigidBody.velocity on client.
    //   B) POSTFIX (server-side, safety net): Force AnimateAuthority()
    //      when HasAuthority && IsOccluded (edge case where server might
    //      occlude entities, preventing WheelTransform sync).
    // =========================================================================
    public static class Bug3_WheelAnimationSyncFix
    {
        // --- Part A: CLIENT-SIDE tread UV fix ---
        // Estimate WheelRpm from synced velocity so Animate()'s UV scroll works.
        // Runs BEFORE PhysicsUpdate's wheel loop where Animate() reads WheelRpm.
        public static void PhysicsUpdate_Prefix(WheeledBase __instance)
        {
            // Only needed on non-authority (client) + visible entities
            if (__instance.HasAuthority || __instance.IsOccluded) return;

            try
            {
                float velocity = __instance.VelocityMagnitude;
                if (velocity < 0.01f) return; // stationary, no UV scroll needed

                // Direction: dot product of rigidbody velocity vs entity forward
                Vector3 rbVel = __instance.RigidBody.velocity;
                float forwardDot = Vector3.Dot(rbVel.normalized, __instance.ThingTransform.forward);
                float sign = forwardDot >= 0f ? 1f : -1f;

                for (int i = 0; i < __instance.Wheels.Count; i++)
                {
                    var wheel = __instance.Wheels[i];
                    // Estimate RPM: rpm = v * 60 / (2π * radius)
                    float radius = 0.3f; // safe default
                    if (wheel.WheelCollider != null)
                    {
                        float r = wheel.WheelCollider.radius;
                        if (r > 0.01f) radius = r;
                    }
                    float rpm = velocity * 60f / (2f * Mathf.PI * radius);
                    wheel.WheelRpm = sign * Mathf.Clamp(rpm, -100f, 100f);
                }

                if (Plugin.ShouldLog($"Bug3-UV-{__instance.GetInstanceID()}", 10f))
                    Plugin.Log.LogInfo($"[Bug3] TreadUV: vel={velocity:F2}, dot={forwardDot:F2}, rpm={__instance.Wheels[0].WheelRpm:F1}, wheels={__instance.Wheels.Count}");
            }
            catch (Exception ex)
            {
                if (Plugin.ShouldLog("Bug3-UV-err", 30f))
                    Plugin.Log.LogError($"[Bug3] TreadUV prefix error: {ex.Message}");
            }
        }

        // --- Part B: SERVER-SIDE safety net ---
        // Force AnimateAuthority when server has IsOccluded=true (edge case).
        private static int _forceCount = 0;

        public static void PhysicsUpdate_Postfix(WheeledBase __instance)
        {
            if (Plugin.ShouldLog($"Bug3-Phys-{__instance.GetInstanceID()}", 10f))
                Plugin.Log.LogInfo($"[Bug3] PhysicsUpdate: type={__instance.GetType().Name}, HasAuth={__instance.HasAuthority}, IsOccluded={__instance.IsOccluded}, wheels={__instance.Wheels.Count}, forced={_forceCount}");

            // Only act when original code skipped AnimateAuthority
            if (!__instance.HasAuthority || !__instance.IsOccluded)
                return;

            _forceCount++;
            for (int i = 0; i < __instance.Wheels.Count; i++)
            {
                try
                {
                    __instance.Wheels[i].AnimateAuthority();
                }
                catch (Exception ex)
                {
                    Plugin.Log.LogError($"[Bug3] AnimateAuthority[{i}] error: {ex.Message}");
                }
            }
        }
    }

    // =========================================================================
    // BUG 4: Roam() regression - AIMeE stops roaming when no ore found
    //
    // Root cause: In the Orbital Update, Roam() was refactored. When
    // _minableDataQueue.Count <= 0 after scanning, the method now does:
    //     if (_minableDataQueue.Count <= 0) return;
    // This early return skips ALL driving logic (steering, motor, obstacle
    // avoidance). In the old code, execution fell through to the driving
    // section, so AIMeE would continue roaming while periodically rescanning.
    //
    // Additionally, MinableSearchArea was halved from 32 to 16, reducing
    // the ore detection range. We restore it to 32 in Plugin.Awake().
    //
    // Fix: Postfix on Roam() that applies the vanilla driving logic when
    // the early return occurred (queue empty, not storage full).
    // =========================================================================
    public static class Bug4_RoamNoOreFix
    {
        private static readonly FieldInfo QueueField =
            AccessTools.Field(typeof(RobotMining), "_minableDataQueue");
        private static readonly FieldInfo ReversingField =
            AccessTools.Field(typeof(RobotMining), "_reversing");
        private static readonly FieldInfo RoamTimeoutField =
            AccessTools.Field(typeof(RobotMining), "_roamTimeout");
        private static readonly FieldInfo DeltaField =
            AccessTools.Field(typeof(RobotMining), "Delta");

        /// <summary>
        /// When Roam() returned early due to empty ore queue, apply driving
        /// logic so AIMeE continues roaming and searching for ore veins.
        /// Replicates the vanilla obstacle avoidance + random steering + motor.
        /// </summary>
        public static void Roam_Postfix(RobotMining __instance)
        {
            try
            {
                // Only act if Roam() hit the empty-queue early return
                var queue = QueueField.GetValue(__instance) as System.Collections.IList;
                if (queue == null || queue.Count > 0) return; // ore found, vanilla handled it
                if (__instance.IsStorageFull) return; // storage full path handled it
                if (!__instance.OnOff || !__instance.Powered) return;

                float power = __instance.Power;
                float velocity = __instance.VelocityMagnitude;
                float maxSpeed = __instance.MaxSpeed;
                float reversing = (float)ReversingField.GetValue(__instance);

                // Obstacle detection: raycast forward
                Vector3 worldCoM = __instance.RigidBody.worldCenterOfMass;
                if (reversing <= 0f)
                {
                    RaycastHit hit;
                    int mask = ~0; // default all layers
                    try
                    {
                        if (CursorManager.Instance != null)
                            mask = CursorManager.Instance.TerrainHitMask;
                    }
                    catch { }
                    if (Physics.Raycast(worldCoM, __instance.ThingTransform.forward, out hit, 0.2f, mask))
                    {
                        reversing = 5f;
                    }
                }

                // Reversing: drive backwards
                if (reversing > 0f)
                {
                    float speedRatio = Mathf.Clamp01(velocity / maxSpeed);
                    __instance.TargetMotorPower = -(power * (1f - speedRatio));
                    reversing -= Time.deltaTime;
                    ReversingField.SetValue(__instance, reversing);
                    return;
                }
                ReversingField.SetValue(__instance, reversing);

                // Random steering changes every 1-3 seconds
                float roamTimeout = (float)RoamTimeoutField.GetValue(__instance);
                if (roamTimeout <= 0f)
                {
                    roamTimeout = UnityEngine.Random.Range(1f, 3f);
                    float delta = UnityEngine.Random.Range(-15f, 15f);
                    DeltaField.SetValue(__instance, delta);
                    __instance.TargetSteeringAngle = delta;
                }
                roamTimeout -= Time.deltaTime;
                RoamTimeoutField.SetValue(__instance, roamTimeout);

                // Forward motor power (speed-limited)
                float speedRatio2 = Mathf.Clamp01(velocity / maxSpeed);
                __instance.TargetMotorPower = power * (1f - speedRatio2);
                __instance.TargetBrakePower = velocity > maxSpeed
                    ? power * Mathf.InverseLerp(maxSpeed, maxSpeed * 1.3f, velocity)
                    : 0f;

                if (Plugin.ShouldLog($"Bug4-Roam-{__instance.GetInstanceID()}", 10f))
                    Plugin.Log.LogInfo($"[Bug4] Roam no-ore drive: vel={velocity:F2}, motor={__instance.TargetMotorPower:F4}, steer={__instance.TargetSteeringAngle:F1}, reversing={reversing:F1}");
            }
            catch (Exception ex)
            {
                if (Plugin.ShouldLog("Bug4-err", 30f))
                    Plugin.Log.LogError($"[Bug4] Roam postfix error: {ex.Message}");
            }
        }
    }
}
```

- oldString:

```
    // =========================================================================
    // BUG 3: Wheel animations don't sync on dedicated server
    //
    // Root cause: In WheeledBase.PhysicsUpdate(), AnimateAuthority() is gated
    // by !IsOccluded. On a dedicated server there is no camera, so the
    // occlusion system may mark entities as occluded. However,
    // AnimateAuthority() is the ONLY method that syncs WheelCollider physics
    // state → WheelTransform visual state. RoverUpdate (network sync struct)
    // captures WheelTransform.localEulerAngles. If AnimateAuthority doesn't
    // run, clients receive stale/zero rotation data → frozen wheels.
    //
    // Note: GameManager.IsBatchMode is FALSE on the dedicated server, so we
    // cannot use it to detect headless mode. Instead, we simply ensure
    // AnimateAuthority() runs when HasAuthority && IsOccluded (the case the
    // original code skips). This is safe on both client and server:
    //   - Server: ensures WheelTransform data for network sync
    //   - Client with occluded entity: negligible cost, entity not visible
    //   - Client with visible entity: original code already handles it
    // =========================================================================
    public static class Bug3_WheelAnimationSyncFix
    {
        /// <summary>
        /// After PhysicsUpdate, if the original code skipped AnimateAuthority
        /// because IsOccluded was true, call it anyway for authoritative
        /// entities. This ensures WheelTransform data is always up-to-date
        /// for network sync via RoverUpdate.
        /// </summary>
        // Track how many times we've actually forced AnimateAuthority
        private static int _forceCount = 0;

        public static void PhysicsUpdate_Postfix(WheeledBase __instance)
        {
            string logKey = $"Bug3-Phys-{__instance.GetInstanceID()}";

            // Log state for ALL WheeledBase instances (not just when condition met)
            if (Plugin.ShouldLog(logKey, 10f))
            {
                Plugin.Log.LogInfo($"[Bug3] PhysicsUpdate: type={__instance.GetType().Name}, HasAuth={__instance.HasAuthority}, IsOccluded={__instance.IsOccluded}, Wheels={__instance.Wheels.Count}, forced={_forceCount}");
            }

            // Original code path: HasAuthority → Apply() + if(!IsOccluded) AnimateAuthority()
            // We only need to act when original skipped AnimateAuthority
            if (!__instance.HasAuthority || !__instance.IsOccluded)
                return;

            _forceCount++;
            if (Plugin.ShouldLog(logKey + "-force", 5f))
                Plugin.Log.LogInfo($"[Bug3] FORCING AnimateAuthority (HasAuth=true, IsOccluded=true, wheels={__instance.Wheels.Count})");

            for (int i = 0; i < __instance.Wheels.Count; i++)
            {
                try
                {
                    __instance.Wheels[i].AnimateAuthority();
                }
                catch (Exception ex)
                {
                    Plugin.Log.LogError($"[Bug3] AnimateAuthority[{i}] threw: {ex.Message}");
                }
            }
        }
    }
}
```

- newString:

```
# AIMeE (RobotMining) Bug Report — Orbital Update

**Affects**: Multiplayer (dedicated server + client)  
**Class**: `Assets.Scripts.Objects.RobotMining` / `Assets.Scripts.Vehicles.WheeledBase`  
**Severity**: All bugs make AIMeE non-functional or visually broken in multiplayer

---

## Bug 1: AIMeE stops mining after first ore in Roam mode (Mode 3) — CONFIRMED FIXED

**Observed**: AIMeE enters Roam (Mode 3), mines one ore, then permanently refuses to mine again. Re-sending mode 3 via IC10 has no effect. 

**Root cause**: `_mineCancellation.Initialized` is never reset after `Mine()` completes.

`MovingToMineable()` calls `_mineCancellation.CancelAndInitialize()` before launching async `Mine()`. This sets `Initialized = true`. After `Mine()` finishes, `Cancel()` is **never called** — the only `Cancel()` call is in `OnDestroy()`.

Every subsequent call to `MovingToMineable()` hits this guard and returns immediately:
```csharp
// MovingToMineable(), line ~1079
if (this._mineCancellation.Initialized) return true;  // ← Stuck here forever
```

**Secondary**: `OnChildEnterInventory()` (chip swap) resets `TargetMinable` but does **not** cancel `_mineCancellation` or call `ClearMinableQueue()`. Stale `_minableDataQueue` entries and `Vein.AllAimeeQueuedMinables` hashes persist across chip swaps.

**Regression from**: Old synchronous `Mine()` used animation callbacks (`OnCustomImportFinished`/`OnCustomExportFinished`) that self-reset state. The new async `UniTaskVoid Mine()` has no completion handler.

**Fix (Harmony)**: Call `_mineCancellation.Cancel()` in `OnMinedOre()` postfix. Also cancel + `ClearMinableQueue()` on chip swap in `OnChildEnterInventory()` postfix. Safety timeout in `MovingToMineable()` prefix resets after 30s.

**Status**: ✅ Confirmed working on dedicated server — AIMeE mines continuously.

---

## Bug 2: AIMeE hops/teleports when off but receiving logic — CONFIRMED FIXED

**Observed**: AIMeE is turned off but an IC10 or logic transmitter continues setting Mode to 1–5. The robot periodically hops into the air and sometimes teleports to a random nearby position.

**Root cause**: `UpdateEachFrame()` has **no `OnOff`/`Powered` guard** before the mode switch.

While `MoveTowards()` checks `(!OnOff || !Powered || Error > 0)` and returns false, two methods called from the mode switch do not:

1. **`TryUnstuck()`** (called in modes 1, 2, 3, 5): Checks if robot moved < 0.1 units in 60 seconds. A powered-off robot always fails this check → calls `Unstuck()` → **writes `ThingTransformPosition` directly** to a random point ~2 units away = **teleport**.

2. **`PathToTarget()`** (mode 5): Applies `RigidBody.AddForce(Vector3.up * Random.Range(0.9f, 5f), ForceMode.VelocityChange)` when stationary = **hop**.

**Fix (Harmony)**: Prefix on `TryUnstuck()` and `PathToTarget()` returning false when `!OnOff || !Powered`. Postfix on `UpdateEachFrame()` enforcing brakes when off.

**Status**: ✅ Confirmed working on dedicated server — AIMeE stays still when powered off.

---

## Bug 3: Tread animations frozen on multiplayer clients — FIX DEPLOYED

**Observed**: When AIMeE moves in multiplayer, clients see the robot moving but treads are completely frozen. The treads animate correctly in single player and on the server.

**Root cause (revised after server testing)**:

The original hypothesis (server `IsOccluded` blocking `AnimateAuthority`) was partially correct for an edge case, but the **primary issue** is on the client:

1. `WheeledBase.PhysicsUpdate()` calls `EnableWheelColliders(HasAuthority)` every frame
2. On the client (`HasAuthority=false`), this **disables all WheelColliders**
3. `WheelAudio()` calls `WheelCollider.GetGroundHit()` → returns `false` on disabled collider → **early returns without updating `WheelRpm`**
4. `Animate()` (client path) reads `WheelRpm` for UV texture scrolling: `num = WheelRpm / 60f * UvOffsetScale`
5. `WheelRpm = 0` → `num = 0` → `_lastOffset` never changes → **frozen tread UV**
6. `RoverUpdate` (network sync struct) only syncs `WheelTransform.localEulerAngles` — it does NOT sync `WheelRpm` or UV offset data

AIMeE's treads use UV texture scrolling (`Material.SetTextureOffset`) rather than geometric wheel rotation, so even though WheelTransform rotation is synced, the visual tread effect is completely absent.

**Fix (Harmony)**:
- **Part A (Client prefix)**: Before `PhysicsUpdate`'s wheel loop, estimate `WheelRpm` from `VelocityMagnitude` (which IS synced via `PhysicsUpdateMessage.Velocity`). Formula: `rpm = velocity × 60 / (2π × radius)`. Direction from `RigidBody.velocity · ThingTransform.forward`.
- **Part B (Server postfix, safety net)**: When `HasAuthority && IsOccluded`, force `AnimateAuthority()` to ensure `WheelTransform` data stays current for network sync.

**Status**: 🔧 Fix deployed, awaiting visual confirmation on client.

---

## Bug 4: Roam regression — AIMeE stops exploring when no ore found — FIX DEPLOYED

**Observed**: AIMeE used to roam farther and farther when no ore was nearby. In the Orbital Update, she stops moving entirely when no ore is within scan range.

**Root cause**: Two changes in Roam():

1. **Early return added**: When `_minableDataQueue.Count <= 0` after scanning, the new code does `return;` — this skips all driving logic (steering, motor, obstacle avoidance). In the old code, execution fell through to the driving section, allowing AIMeE to continue roaming while periodically rescanning.

2. **`MinableSearchArea` halved**: Changed from `32` to `16`, reducing ore detection range by half.

```csharp
// NEW code - Orbital Update
if (this._minableDataQueue.Count <= 0)
{
    return;  // ← Bug: skips ALL driving logic below
}
// ... driving logic never reached when no ore nearby
```

**Fix (Harmony)**: Postfix on `Roam()` that applies the vanilla driving logic (obstacle avoidance + random steering + motor power) when the `_minableDataQueue` is empty and storage isn't full. Also restores `MinableSearchArea` to `32` via reflection in plugin init.

**Status**: 🔧 Fix deployed, awaiting behavioral confirmation.
```

- oldString:

```
# AIMeE (RobotMining) Bug Report — Orbital Update

**Affects**: Multiplayer (dedicated server + client)  
**Class**: `Assets.Scripts.Objects.RobotMining` / `Assets.Scripts.Vehicles.WheeledBase`  
**Severity**: All three bugs make AIMeE non-functional in multiplayer

---

## Bug 1: AIMeE stops mining after first ore in Roam mode (Mode 3)

**Observed**: AIMeE enters Roam (Mode 3), mines one ore, then permanently refuses to mine again. Re-sending mode 3 via IC10 has no effect. 

**Root cause**: `_mineCancellation.Initialized` is never reset after `Mine()` completes.

`MovingToMineable()` calls `_mineCancellation.CancelAndInitialize()` before launching async `Mine()`. This sets `Initialized = true`. After `Mine()` finishes, `Cancel()` is **never called** — the only `Cancel()` call is in `OnDestroy()`.

Every subsequent call to `MovingToMineable()` hits this guard and returns immediately:
```csharp
// MovingToMineable(), line ~1079
if (this._mineCancellation.Initialized) return true;  // ← Stuck here forever
```

**Secondary**: `OnChildEnterInventory()` (chip swap) resets `TargetMinable` but does **not** cancel `_mineCancellation` or call `ClearMinableQueue()`. Stale `_minableDataQueue` entries and `Vein.AllAimeeQueuedMinables` hashes persist across chip swaps.

**Regression from**: Old synchronous `Mine()` used animation callbacks (`OnCustomImportFinished`/`OnCustomExportFinished`) that self-reset state. The new async `UniTaskVoid Mine()` has no completion handler.

**Fix**: Call `_mineCancellation.Cancel()` after `Mine()` completes (e.g. add it to `OnMinedOre()` or a `finally` block in the async method). Also call `Cancel()` + `ClearMinableQueue()` in `OnChildEnterInventory()`.

---

## Bug 2: AIMeE hops/teleports when off but receiving logic

**Observed**: AIMeE is turned off but an IC10 or logic transmitter continues setting Mode to 1–5. The robot periodically hops into the air and sometimes teleports to a random nearby position.

**Root cause**: `UpdateEachFrame()` has **no `OnOff`/`Powered` guard** before the mode switch.

While `MoveTowards()` checks `(!OnOff || !Powered || Error > 0)` and returns false, two methods called from the mode switch do not:

1. **`TryUnstuck()`** (called in modes 1, 2, 3, 5): Checks if robot moved < 0.1 units in 60 seconds. A powered-off robot always fails this check → calls `Unstuck()` → **writes `ThingTransformPosition` directly** to a random point ~2 units away = **teleport**.

2. **`PathToTarget()`** (mode 5): Applies `RigidBody.AddForce(Vector3.up * Random.Range(0.9f, 5f), ForceMode.VelocityChange)` when stationary = **hop**.

**Fix**: Add `if (!OnOff || !Powered) return;` at the top of the mode switch in `UpdateEachFrame()`, after the `RunSimulation` check.

---

## Bug 3: Wheel animations don't play on dedicated server

**Observed**: When AIMeE moves on a dedicated server, clients see the robot moving but with completely frozen wheels. Works correctly in single player.

**Root cause**: In `WheeledBase.PhysicsUpdate()`, `AnimateAuthority()` is gated by `!IsOccluded`:

```csharp
if (this.HasAuthority)
{
    wheel.Apply(motor, brake, steer);      // Physics always runs
    if (!this.IsOccluded)                  // ← Visual rendering gate
    {
        wheel.AnimateAuthority();          // Syncs WheelCollider → WheelTransform
    }
}
```

`AnimateAuthority()` is the **only** method that copies `WheelCollider.GetWorldPose()` → `WheelTransform`. `RoverUpdate` (network sync struct) reads `WheelTransform.localEulerAngles` to send to clients.

On a dedicated server (`GameManager.IsBatchMode = true`), there is no camera → `InventoryManager.Parent` is null → `SetOcclusion()` returns early → `IsOccluded` may default to an incorrect state, preventing `AnimateAuthority()` from running → `RoverUpdate` captures stale zero rotation data → clients receive frozen wheel rotations.

Note: `RobotMining` does **not** override `HasAuthority` (unlike `Rover` which has driver-aware logic). On the server, `HasAuthority` is always true via `Thing.HasAuthority` (`RunSimulation = true`).

**Fix**: When `HasAuthority && IsBatchMode`, always call `AnimateAuthority()` regardless of `IsOccluded`. Occlusion is a rendering optimization irrelevant to a headless server, but the `WheelTransform` data is required for network sync.
```


</details></tool-use>

- command:

```
# Copy to dedicated server mods folder
$serverMods = "C:\Server\Stationeers\mods\AimeeBugFixes"
if (!(Test-Path $serverMods)) { New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path $serverMods -Force | Out-Null }
if (!(Test-Path "$serverMods\About")) { New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path "$serverMods\About" -Force | Out-Null }
Copy-Item "bin\AimeeBugFixes.dll" -Destination $serverMods -Force
Copy-Item "About\About.xml" -Destination "$serverMods\About" -Force
Write-Host "Deployed to server: $serverMods"
Get-ChildItem $serverMods -Recurse | Format-Table Name, Length, LastWriteTime

_**User (2026-02-07 17:15:47Z)**_

Why do the tread animations work on single player but not multiplayer then?

---

_**Agent (copilot/claude-opus-4.6)**_



 It comes down to **two different code paths** in `WheeledBase.PhysicsUpdate()`:

**Single player** (`HasAuthority = true`):
1. `EnableWheelColliders(true)` — WheelColliders are **enabled**
2. `wheel.AnimateAuthority()` runs — reads `WheelCollider.rpm` directly from the **active physics engine** and applies UV scrolling
3. Works perfectly because `WheelCollider.rpm` is a live value computed by Unity's wheel physics

**Multiplayer client** (`HasAuthority = false`):
1. `EnableWheelColliders(false)` — WheelColliders are **disabled** (intentionally, to prevent physics conflicts with the server)
2. `wheel.Animate(rotation)` runs instead — applies the synced `WheelTransform.localEulerAngles` (geometric rotation), then tries to UV scroll using `WheelRpm`
3. `wheel.WheelAudio()` is the only thing that sets `WheelRpm`, but its first line is `WheelCollider.GetGroundHit()` — which returns `false` on a disabled collider → **early return, `WheelRpm` never updated**
4. `WheelRpm` stays at `0` → UV offset calculation produces `0` → frozen treads

The network sync (`RoverUpdate`) only transmits `WheelTransform.localEulerAngles` per wheel — it doesn't include `WheelRpm` or any UV data. So the client has no way to know the tread speed.

It's a vanilla game bug: the devs disabled WheelColliders on clients (correct for physics), but the client-side animation path depends on `WheelCollider.rpm` to drive the tread UV scrolling. The data source was cut off but no alternative was provided.

Our fix estimates `WheelRpm` from `VelocityMagnitude` (which IS synced via `PhysicsUpdateMessage.Velocity` → `RigidBody.velocity`) before `Animate()` reads it.

---

_**User (2026-02-07 18:41:24Z)**_

Why are the devs disabling the wheel colliders on aimee?

---

_**Agent (copilot/claude-opus-4.6)**_



 It's not AIMeE-specific — `EnableWheelColliders(HasAuthority)` is in `WheeledBase.PhysicsUpdate()`, which runs for **all** wheeled vehicles (Rovers too).

It's standard multiplayer physics architecture: in an authoritative server model, only the **server** (authority) runs the "real" physics simulation. If clients also had active WheelColliders, you'd get:

1. **Dual simulation conflicts** — Unity's WheelCollider applies forces to the Rigidbody (motor torque, braking, suspension). The client is also receiving server-authoritative position/velocity via `PhysicsUpdateMessage` and lerping the Rigidbody toward those values. Two competing sources of movement = jittering, rubber-banding, desync.

2. **Input mismatch** — The server is running `wheel.Apply(motor, brake, steer)` based on the authoritative `CurrentMotorPower`/`CurrentBrakePower`/`CurrentSteeringAngle`. The client doesn't have the same values at the same time (network latency). Active WheelColliders with stale/zero inputs would fight the server-synced position.

3. **Ground hit conflicts** — WheelCollider does suspension raycasts and ground contact detection. On the client, the terrain/physics state might differ slightly from the server, causing the vehicle to bounce or clip differently.

So disabling them is correct. The client just puppets the visual transforms from network data (`RoverUpdate` → `WheelTransform.localEulerAngles`, `PhysicsUpdateMessage` → `RigidBody.position/velocity`).

The oversight is that they also depend on `WheelCollider.rpm` (indirectly via `WheelAudio()` → `WheelRpm`) for the UV tread animation on the client path, and nobody added a fallback for when the collider is disabled. Probably worked fine when all wheeled vehicles were Rovers with geometric wheel rotation (which IS synced), and broke when AIMeE was added with UV-scrolling treads.

---

_**Agent (copilot/claude-opus-4.6)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The treads are spinning now (the UV fix is working!), but they won't stop because of a bug in our prefix — when velocity drops below the threshold, we just `return` without resetting `WheelRpm` to 0. The stale value persists because `WheelAudio()` (the only other thing that writes `WheelRpm`) early-returns on disabled WheelColliders. So the last non-zero RPM lingers forever.

Also missing: smoothing. The original code uses `Mathf.SmoothDamp` to ease RPM changes; we slam it to the computed value instantly, causing the "full speed" jumps.
</details></think>

- newString:

```
        // --- Part A: CLIENT-SIDE tread UV fix ---
        // Estimate WheelRpm from synced velocity so Animate()'s UV scroll works.
        // Runs BEFORE PhysicsUpdate's wheel loop where Animate() reads WheelRpm.
        // Uses SmoothDamp to match vanilla WheelAudio() smoothing behavior.
        private static readonly Dictionary<int, float> _smoothVelocity = new Dictionary<int, float>();
        private const float RpmSmoothTime = 0.1f; // matches Wheel._rpmSmoothTime

        public static void PhysicsUpdate_Prefix(WheeledBase __instance)
        {
            // Only needed on non-authority (client) + visible entities
            if (__instance.HasAuthority || __instance.IsOccluded) return;

            try
            {
                float velocity = __instance.VelocityMagnitude;

                // Direction: dot product of rigidbody velocity vs entity forward
                float sign = 1f;
                if (velocity > 0.01f)
                {
                    Vector3 rbVel = __instance.RigidBody.velocity;
                    float forwardDot = Vector3.Dot(rbVel.normalized, __instance.ThingTransform.forward);
                    sign = forwardDot >= 0f ? 1f : -1f;
                }

                for (int i = 0; i < __instance.Wheels.Count; i++)
                {
                    var wheel = __instance.Wheels[i];

                    // Estimate target RPM from velocity (or 0 when stopped)
                    float targetRpm = 0f;
                    if (velocity > 0.01f)
                    {
                        float radius = 0.3f; // safe default
                        if (wheel.WheelCollider != null)
                        {
                            float r = wheel.WheelCollider.radius;
                            if (r > 0.01f) radius = r;
                        }
                        targetRpm = sign * Mathf.Clamp(velocity * 60f / (2f * Mathf.PI * radius), -100f, 100f);
                    }

                    // SmoothDamp toward target (same approach as vanilla WheelAudio)
                    int key = wheel.GetHashCode();
                    if (!_smoothVelocity.TryGetValue(key, out float sv)) sv = 0f;
                    wheel.WheelRpm = Mathf.SmoothDamp(wheel.WheelRpm, targetRpm, ref sv, RpmSmoothTime);
                    _smoothVelocity[key] = sv;

                    // Snap to zero when close enough to avoid infinite tiny drift
                    if (Mathf.Abs(wheel.WheelRpm) < 0.5f && Mathf.Abs(targetRpm) < 0.01f)
                        wheel.WheelRpm = 0f;
                }

                if (Plugin.ShouldLog($"Bug3-UV-{__instance.GetInstanceID()}", 10f))
                    Plugin.Log.LogInfo($"[Bug3] TreadUV: vel={velocity:F2}, rpm={__instance.Wheels[0].WheelRpm:F1}, wheels={__instance.Wheels.Count}");
            }
            catch (Exception ex)
            {
                if (Plugin.ShouldLog("Bug3-UV-err", 30f))
                    Plugin.Log.LogError($"[Bug3] TreadUV prefix error: {ex.Message}");
            }
        }
```

- oldString:

```
        // --- Part A: CLIENT-SIDE tread UV fix ---
        // Estimate WheelRpm from synced velocity so Animate()'s UV scroll works.
        // Runs BEFORE PhysicsUpdate's wheel loop where Animate() reads WheelRpm.
        public static void PhysicsUpdate_Prefix(WheeledBase __instance)
        {
            // Only needed on non-authority (client) + visible entities
            if (__instance.HasAuthority || __instance.IsOccluded) return;

            try
            {
                float velocity = __instance.VelocityMagnitude;
                if (velocity < 0.01f) return; // stationary, no UV scroll needed

                // Direction: dot product of rigidbody velocity vs entity forward
                Vector3 rbVel = __instance.RigidBody.velocity;
                float forwardDot = Vector3.Dot(rbVel.normalized, __instance.ThingTransform.forward);
                float sign = forwardDot >= 0f ? 1f : -1f;

                for (int i = 0; i < __instance.Wheels.Count; i++)
                {
                    var wheel = __instance.Wheels[i];
                    // Estimate RPM: rpm = v * 60 / (2π * radius)
                    float radius = 0.3f; // safe default
                    if (wheel.WheelCollider != null)
                    {
                        float r = wheel.WheelCollider.radius;
                        if (r > 0.01f) radius = r;
                    }
                    float rpm = velocity * 60f / (2f * Mathf.PI * radius);
                    wheel.WheelRpm = sign * Mathf.Clamp(rpm, -100f, 100f);
                }

                if (Plugin.ShouldLog($"Bug3-UV-{__instance.GetInstanceID()}", 10f))
                    Plugin.Log.LogInfo($"[Bug3] TreadUV: vel={velocity:F2}, dot={forwardDot:F2}, rpm={__instance.Wheels[0].WheelRpm:F1}, wheels={__instance.Wheels.Count}");
            }
            catch (Exception ex)
            {
                if (Plugin.ShouldLog("Bug3-UV-err", 30f))
                    Plugin.Log.LogError($"[Bug3] TreadUV prefix error: {ex.Message}");
            }
        }
```

```
        public static void PhysicsUpdate_Prefix(WheeledBase __instance)
        {
            // Only needed on non-authority (client) + visible entities
            if (__instance.HasAuthority || __instance.IsOccluded) return;

            try
            {
                // Use RigidBody.velocity directly — VelocityMagnitude may be stale
                // on non-authority clients (updated via OnThreadUpdate/CacheVelocity
                // which may not run on the client physics path).
                Vector3 rbVel = __instance.RigidBody.velocity;
                float velocity = rbVel.magnitude;

                // Dead zone: network lerping and physics jitter produce small
                // residual velocities even when stopped. AIMeE MaxSpeed=3, so
                // 0.15 m/s is safely below any intentional movement.
                float sign = 1f;
                float targetRpm = 0f;
                if (velocity > 0.15f)
                {
                    float forwardDot = Vector3.Dot(rbVel.normalized, __instance.ThingTransform.forward);
                    sign = forwardDot >= 0f ? 1f : -1f;

                    for (int i = 0; i < __instance.Wheels.Count; i++)
                    {
                        var wheel = __instance.Wheels[i];
                        float radius = 0.3f;
                        if (wheel.WheelCollider != null)
                        {
                            float r = wheel.WheelCollider.radius;
                            if (r > 0.01f) radius = r;
                        }
                        targetRpm = sign * Mathf.Clamp(velocity * 60f / (2f * Mathf.PI * radius), -100f, 100f);

                        int key = wheel.GetHashCode();
                        if (!_smoothVelocity.TryGetValue(key, out float sv)) sv = 0f;
                        wheel.WheelRpm = Mathf.SmoothDamp(wheel.WheelRpm, targetRpm, ref sv, RpmSmoothTime);
                        _smoothVelocity[key] = sv;
                    }
                }
                else
                {
                    // Stopped: drive all wheels to zero RPM
                    for (int i = 0; i < __instance.Wheels.Count; i++)
                    {
                        var wheel = __instance.Wheels[i];
                        int key = wheel.GetHashCode();
                        if (!_smoothVelocity.TryGetValue(key, out float sv)) sv = 0f;
                        wheel.WheelRpm = Mathf.SmoothDamp(wheel.WheelRpm, 0f, ref sv, RpmSmoothTime);
                        _smoothVelocity[key] = sv;

                        // Hard snap to zero when nearly stopped
                        if (Mathf.Abs(wheel.WheelRpm) < 1f)
                            wheel.WheelRpm = 0f;
                    }
                }

                if (Plugin.ShouldLog($"Bug3-UV-{__instance.GetInstanceID()}", 10f))
                    Plugin.Log.LogInfo($"[Bug3] TreadUV: rbVel={velocity:F3}, rpm={__instance.Wheels[0].WheelRpm:F1}, wheels={__instance.Wheels.Count}");
            }
            catch (Exception ex)
            {
                if (Plugin.ShouldLog("Bug3-UV-err", 30f))
                    Plugin.Log.LogError($"[Bug3] TreadUV prefix error: {ex.Message}");
            }
        }
```

_**User (2026-02-08 04:48:24Z)**_

Please add the logging and also there is an issue when you grab/pick up an aimee the game sends you flying upward for some reason.

---

_**Agent (copilot/claude-opus-4.6)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
That `AddForce(vector, VelocityChange)` at line 1174 is the PathToTarget hop we already blocked with Bug2. Let me look at the parent class for pickup/drag behavior:  

The drag system uses a ConfigurableJoint. The flying upward is probably from the TryUnstuck/PathToTarget addForce while being dragged. But our Bug2 fix only blocks when OnOff is false. Let me check if the mode stays active while being dragged: 

Now let me look at Follow() — the mode that activates when a player is close, which would be the case when dragging: 

Now let me check what happens with WheeledBase when being dragged — the `PhysicsUpdate` still runs and Apply() still applies motor torque via WheelColliders even while being dragged. The real issue is when AIMeE is in mode 1 (Follow) or mode 3 (Roam) - she actively drives her motors while attached to the player via a ConfigurableJoint. The motor torque + TryUnstuck teleport could launch the player.

Let me check if the WheeledBase PhysicsUpdate has any drag check and look at what `_isStuckCheckAmount` is: 

Now let me check — when AIMeE is being dragged, `ParentSlot` is non-null (she's in the player's drag slot). The `IsChild` property should be true. Let me check if `UpdateEachFrame` or `PhysicsUpdate` checks that:  

So `UpdateEachFrame` has NO check for being dragged. She's still running Follow/Roam/etc while attached to the player via a joint. The wheels are spinning, motor torque is being applied to the player's rigidbody through the joint, and TryUnstuck could teleport her (and the connected player) after 60 seconds.

But the problem says the player flies upward immediately when grabbing. Let me look more carefully — the `PathToTarget` AddForce is only mode 5. For the immediate upward launch, it's more likely the **WheeledBase physics**: when the player grabs AIMeE, the joint connects two rigidbodies. AIMeE's WheelColliders are still applying motor torque. The `DragInSlot` code changes AIMeE's mass (`_startMass * DraggedMassScale * massRatio`), potentially making her very light while the motors are still applying full torque. Combined with the joint, this could launch both entities.

Let me check `DraggedMassScale`: 

There it is. When dragging, AIMeE's mass is reduced to **10% × (playerMass/aimeeMass)**. If AIMeE weighs 5kg and the player weighs 70kg, the ratio is 14× — but multiplied by 0.1f, so net mass = 5 × 0.1 × 14 = 7kg. Meanwhile AIMeE's WheelCollider motor torque is unchanged. And if she's in Roam/Follow mode, `MoveTowards` is actively driving her toward the player (who IS the drag parent). The motor force through the joint would push the player.

But the "flying upward" specifically suggests the `PathToTarget` AddForce(`Vector3.up * Random.Range(0.9, 5)`) or the `Unstuck()` teleport. Since PathToTarget is mode 5 and TryUnstuck takes 60s, the immediate upward launch is likely from the joint physics itself — AIMeE's WheelCollider suspension springs applying force through the joint.

The fix: when AIMeE is being dragged (has a `ParentSlot` / `Joint`), disable motor power and set brakes. Let me now implement both fixes: 

Now let me check what fields/types we need for the new patches:  

Good. `Joint` is a `CharacterJoint` and `ParentSlot` is public. Now let me also check the `AllAimeeQueuedMinables` field accessibility for the diagnostic:  

Good — `AllAimeeQueuedMinables` is public static. We can access it directly via `Vein.AllAimeeQueuedMinables.Count`. Now let me check the namespace for Vein:   

Now let me read the current end of Plugin.cs so I know the exact structure to add to:  

Now I have the full picture. Let me implement all three changes:
1. Add ore queue diagnostic logging to the Roam postfix
2. Add Bug5 - drag/pickup flying fix
3. Add the new patch registration and using directive   

Now let me also add a call to the ore diagnostics from the Roam_Postfix (for when the queue IS populated and vanilla handles it): 

Good. Now let me also add ore diagnostic logging for the case where the queue IS populated (vanilla handled it), by hooking into Roam_Postfix where `queue.Count > 0`: 

Now let me also add the Bug5 patch to TryUnstuck to prevent teleporting while dragged: 

Now let me check the existing TryUnstuck handling - the Bug2 already blocks TryUnstuck when off. I should add the drag check there too: 

Now let me verify the full file compiles correctly by checking for errors: 

Now let me also check the Bug2 UpdateEachFrame_Postfix doesn't conflict with the new Bug5 UpdateEachFrame_Prefix:  

Good - there's a Bug5 prefix (runs before, can skip) and a Bug2 postfix (runs after). When Bug5 returns false, the postfix will still run. But Bug2's postfix checks `!OnOff || !Powered`, so it won't conflict - if dragged while on, Bug5 already killed motors. If dragged while off, Bug5 still skips, and Bug2 postfix also brakes. That's fine.

Wait - when Bug5 prefix returns `false`, does the postfix still run? By default in Harmony, yes it does. That's fine because Bug2 postfix just adds extra braking when off.

Now let me build: 

Clean build. Now deploy to both server and client: 

Server path doesn't exist on this machine — that's fine, it deployed to the client. Let me also repackage the zip for distribution:  

Now let me update the BUG_REPORT.md with Bug 5:
</details></think>

            TryPatch(ref success, ref fail, "Bug5-DragFix",

    // =========================================================================
    // BUG 5: Picking up / dragging AIMeE launches player into the air
    //
    // Root cause: When a player drags AIMeE, DragInSlot() creates a
    // CharacterJoint between the player and AIMeE rigidbodies, and reduces
    // AIMeE's mass to 10% × (playerMass / aimeeMass). However,
    // UpdateEachFrame() continues running AIMeE's mode logic:
    //   - WheelCollider motor torque is still applied (via PhysicsUpdate)
    //   - Follow() drives toward the nearest player (who IS the dragger)
    //   - TryUnstuck() may teleport both connected bodies
    //   - The joint transfers all forces to the player rigidbody
    // The combination of reduced mass + active motor + joint = player
    // launched upward or sideways.
    //
    // Fix: Skip all mode logic when AIMeE is being dragged (IsChild/Joint).
    // Also brake and zero motor power.
    // =========================================================================
    public static class Bug5_DragFlyingFix
    {
        public static bool UpdateEachFrame_Prefix(RobotMining __instance)
        {
            // If AIMeE is being dragged (in a slot / has a joint), skip all mode logic
            if (__instance.Joint != null || __instance.IsChild)
            {
                // Kill motor, apply brakes so WheelColliders don't push the player
                __instance.TargetMotorPower = 0f;
                __instance.TargetBrakePower = __instance.Power;
                __instance.TargetSteeringAngle = 0f;

                if (Plugin.ShouldLog($"Bug5-Drag-{__instance.GetInstanceID()}", 10f))
                    Plugin.Log.LogInfo($"[Bug5] AIMeE dragged: killing motors (Joint={__instance.Joint != null}, IsChild={__instance.IsChild})");

                return false; // skip original UpdateEachFrame entirely
            }
            return true; // normal execution
        }
    }

    // =========================================================================
    // DIAGNOSTICS: Ore queue health monitoring
    // Logs AllAimeeQueuedMinables hash set size to detect leaks
    // =========================================================================
    public static class OreQueueDiagnostics
    {
        private static readonly FieldInfo QueueField =
            AccessTools.Field(typeof(RobotMining), "_minableDataQueue");

        // Called from Bug4 Roam_Postfix when ore IS found (vanilla handled it)
        public static void LogQueueHealth(RobotMining instance)
        {
            try
            {
                if (!Plugin.ShouldLog($"OreQ-{instance.GetInstanceID()}", 15f)) return;

                var queue = QueueField.GetValue(instance) as System.Collections.IList;
                int queueLen = queue?.Count ?? -1;
                int globalHashes = Vein.AllAimeeQueuedMinables.Count;
                float vel = instance.VelocityMagnitude;

                Plugin.Log.LogInfo($"[OreQ] queue={queueLen}, globalHashes={globalHashes}, vel={vel:F2}, mode={instance.Mode}, pos=({instance.ThingTransformPosition.x:F0},{instance.ThingTransformPosition.z:F0})");
            }
            catch { }
        }
    }
} oldString:    }
}]]
```


</details></tool-use>

```
using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.Reflection;
using Assets.Scripts;
using Assets.Scripts.Networking;
using Assets.Scripts.Objects;
using Assets.Scripts.Objects.Electrical;
using Assets.Scripts.Vehicles;
using BepInEx;
using BepInEx.Logging;
using HarmonyLib;
using UnityEngine;

namespace AimeeBugFixes
{
    [BepInPlugin("com.florpydorp.aimeebugfixes", "AIMeE Bug Fixes", "0.2.0")]
    public class Plugin : BaseUnityPlugin
    {
        internal static ManualLogSource Log;
        private Harmony _harmony;

        // Throttled logging helper: returns true if enough time has passed since last log for this key
        private static readonly Dictionary<string, float> _lastLogTimes = new Dictionary<string, float>();
        internal static bool ShouldLog(string key, float intervalSeconds = 10f)
        {
            float now = Time.time;
            if (!_lastLogTimes.TryGetValue(key, out float lastTime) || (now - lastTime) >= intervalSeconds)
            {
                _lastLogTimes[key] = now;
                return true;
            }
            return false;
        }

        void Awake()
        {
            Log = Logger;
            Log.LogInfo("AIMeE Bug Fixes v0.2.0 loading...");
            Log.LogInfo($"  IsBatchMode={GameManager.IsBatchMode}, RunSimulation={GameManager.RunSimulation}");

            _harmony = new Harmony("com.florpydorp.aimeebugfixes");

            int success = 0;
            int fail = 0;

            // Bug 2: Off-state teleport/hop fix
            TryPatch(ref success, ref fail, "Bug2-TryUnstuck",
                AccessTools.Method(typeof(RobotMining), "TryUnstuck"),
                prefix: new HarmonyMethod(typeof(Bug2_OffStateTeleportFix), nameof(Bug2_OffStateTeleportFix.TryUnstuck_Prefix)));

            TryPatch(ref success, ref fail, "Bug2-PathToTarget",
                AccessTools.Method(typeof(RobotMining), "PathToTarget"),
                prefix: new HarmonyMethod(typeof(Bug2_OffStateTeleportFix), nameof(Bug2_OffStateTeleportFix.PathToTarget_Prefix)));

            TryPatch(ref success, ref fail, "Bug2-UpdateEachFrame",
                AccessTools.Method(typeof(RobotMining), "UpdateEachFrame"),
                postfix: new HarmonyMethod(typeof(Bug2_OffStateTeleportFix), nameof(Bug2_OffStateTeleportFix.UpdateEachFrame_Postfix)));

            // Bug 3: Tread/wheel animation sync on multiplayer clients
            TryPatch(ref success, ref fail, "Bug3-TreadUV",
                AccessTools.Method(typeof(WheeledBase), "PhysicsUpdate"),
                prefix: new HarmonyMethod(typeof(Bug3_WheelAnimationSyncFix), nameof(Bug3_WheelAnimationSyncFix.PhysicsUpdate_Prefix)));

            TryPatch(ref success, ref fail, "Bug3-ServerOccluded",
                AccessTools.Method(typeof(WheeledBase), "PhysicsUpdate"),
                postfix: new HarmonyMethod(typeof(Bug3_WheelAnimationSyncFix), nameof(Bug3_WheelAnimationSyncFix.PhysicsUpdate_Postfix)));

            // Bug 5: Dragging AIMeE launches player - use postfix to let base class
            // logic (UpdatePivotJoint, etc.) run, then zero wheel forces so the drag
            // joint can move AIMeE freely with the player's view.
            TryPatch(ref success, ref fail, "Bug5-DragFix",
                AccessTools.Method(typeof(RobotMining), "UpdateEachFrame"),
                postfix: new HarmonyMethod(typeof(Bug5_DragFlyingFix), nameof(Bug5_DragFlyingFix.UpdateEachFrame_Postfix)));

            Log.LogInfo($"AIMeE Bug Fixes: {success} patches applied, {fail} failed");
            if (fail > 0)
                Log.LogError("Some patches failed! Check errors above.");
        }

        private void TryPatch(ref int success, ref int fail, string name,
            MethodInfo original, HarmonyMethod prefix = null, HarmonyMethod postfix = null)
        {
            try
            {
                if (original == null)
                {
                    Log.LogError($"  [{name}] FAILED - target method not found via reflection");
                    fail++;
                    return;
                }
                _harmony.Patch(original, prefix: prefix, postfix: postfix);
                Log.LogInfo($"  [{name}] OK - patched {original.DeclaringType.Name}.{original.Name}");
                success++;
            }
            catch (Exception ex)
            {
                Log.LogError($"  [{name}] FAILED - {ex.GetType().Name}: {ex.Message}");
                fail++;
            }
        }

        void OnDestroy()
        {
            // Do NOT unpatch here. StationeersLaunchPad destroys plugin
            // GameObjects after chainloader completes, which fires OnDestroy.
            // Harmony patches live in IL and must persist independently.
            Log.LogInfo("AIMeE Bug Fixes: plugin component destroyed (patches remain active)");
        }
    }

    // =========================================================================
    // BUG 2: Off-state hopping/teleporting when receiving logic
    //
    // Root cause: UpdateEachFrame() has no OnOff/Powered guard before the mode
    // switch. When off but an IC10/logic transmitter sets Mode to 1-5:
    //   - TryUnstuck() detects no movement in 60s (off robot barely moves),
    //     calls Unstuck() → writes a random position 2-3 units away (teleport)
    //   - PathToTarget() applies AddForce(Vector3.up * Random.Range(0.9, 5))
    //     causing the robot to hop into the air
    //   - MoveTowards() has its own OnOff guard (returns false), but TryUnstuck
    //     and PathToTarget do not.
    //
    // Also blocks TryUnstuck/PathToTarget while being dragged (Bug 5 support).
    // =========================================================================
    public static class Bug2_OffStateTeleportFix
    {
        public static bool TryUnstuck_Prefix(RobotMining __instance)
        {
            // Bug5: Block TryUnstuck when being dragged (would teleport player+AIMeE)
            if (__instance.Joint != null || __instance.IsChild)
            {
                if (Plugin.ShouldLog($"Bug5-Unstuck-{__instance.GetInstanceID()}", 10f))
                    Plugin.Log.LogInfo($"[Bug5] TryUnstuck: BLOCKED (being dragged)");
                return false;
            }
            if (!__instance.OnOff || !__instance.Powered)
            {
                if (Plugin.ShouldLog($"Bug2-Unstuck-{__instance.GetInstanceID()}", 10f))
                    Plugin.Log.LogInfo($"[Bug2] TryUnstuck: BLOCKED (OnOff={__instance.OnOff}, Powered={__instance.Powered})");
                return false;
            }
            return true;
        }

        public static bool PathToTarget_Prefix(RobotMining __instance)
        {
            // Bug5: Block PathToTarget when being dragged
            if (__instance.Joint != null || __instance.IsChild)
            {
                if (Plugin.ShouldLog($"Bug5-Path-{__instance.GetInstanceID()}", 10f))
                    Plugin.Log.LogInfo($"[Bug5] PathToTarget: BLOCKED (being dragged)");
                return false;
            }
            if (!__instance.OnOff || !__instance.Powered)
            {
                if (Plugin.ShouldLog($"Bug2-Path-{__instance.GetInstanceID()}", 10f))
                    Plugin.Log.LogInfo($"[Bug2] PathToTarget: BLOCKED (OnOff={__instance.OnOff}, Powered={__instance.Powered})");
                return false;
            }
            return true;
        }

        /// <summary>
        /// After UpdateEachFrame, enforce braking when robot is off.
        /// Even though MoveTowards has its own OnOff guard, residual motor
        /// power can still be set by mode methods. This ensures stationary.
        /// </summary>
        public static void UpdateEachFrame_Postfix(RobotMining __instance)
        {
            if (!GameManager.RunSimulation) return;

            if (!__instance.OnOff || !__instance.Powered)
            {
                __instance.TargetBrakePower = __instance.Power;
                __instance.TargetMotorPower = 0f;
            }
        }
    }

    // =========================================================================
    // BUG 3: Tread/wheel UV animation broken on multiplayer clients
    //
    // Root cause:
    //   WheelColliders are DISABLED on non-authority clients
    //   (EnableWheelColliders(HasAuthority) in PhysicsUpdate).
    //   Disabled WheelCollider → GetGroundHit()=false → WheelAudio()
    //   early-returns → WheelRpm never set → Animate() scrolls UV at 0.
    //
    // Fix:
    //   A) PREFIX (client): Estimate WheelRpm from synced RigidBody.velocity
    //   B) POSTFIX (server): Force AnimateAuthority when occluded (safety net)
    // =========================================================================
    public static class Bug3_WheelAnimationSyncFix
    {
        private static readonly Dictionary<int, float> _smoothVelocity = new Dictionary<int, float>();
        private const float RpmSmoothTime = 0.1f; // matches Wheel._rpmSmoothTime

        public static void PhysicsUpdate_Prefix(WheeledBase __instance)
        {
            // Only needed on non-authority (client) + visible entities
            if (__instance.HasAuthority || __instance.IsOccluded) return;

            try
            {
                Vector3 rbVel = __instance.RigidBody.velocity;
                float velocity = rbVel.magnitude;

                // Dead zone: network lerping produces residual velocity when stopped.
                // AIMeE MaxSpeed=3, so 0.15 m/s is safely below intentional movement.
                if (velocity > 0.15f)
                {
                    float forwardDot = Vector3.Dot(rbVel.normalized, __instance.ThingTransform.forward);
                    float sign = forwardDot >= 0f ? 1f : -1f;

                    for (int i = 0; i < __instance.Wheels.Count; i++)
                    {
                        var wheel = __instance.Wheels[i];
                        float radius = 0.3f;
                        if (wheel.WheelCollider != null)
                        {
                            float r = wheel.WheelCollider.radius;
                            if (r > 0.01f) radius = r;
                        }
                        float targetRpm = sign * Mathf.Clamp(velocity * 60f / (2f * Mathf.PI * radius), -100f, 100f);

                        int key = wheel.GetHashCode();
                        if (!_smoothVelocity.TryGetValue(key, out float sv)) sv = 0f;
                        wheel.WheelRpm = Mathf.SmoothDamp(wheel.WheelRpm, targetRpm, ref sv, RpmSmoothTime);
                        _smoothVelocity[key] = sv;
                    }
                }
                else
                {
                    // Stopped: drive all wheels to zero RPM
                    for (int i = 0; i < __instance.Wheels.Count; i++)
                    {
                        var wheel = __instance.Wheels[i];
                        int key = wheel.GetHashCode();
                        if (!_smoothVelocity.TryGetValue(key, out float sv)) sv = 0f;
                        wheel.WheelRpm = Mathf.SmoothDamp(wheel.WheelRpm, 0f, ref sv, RpmSmoothTime);
                        _smoothVelocity[key] = sv;

                        // Hard snap to zero when nearly stopped
                        if (Mathf.Abs(wheel.WheelRpm) < 1f)
                            wheel.WheelRpm = 0f;
                    }
                }
            }
            catch (Exception ex)
            {
                if (Plugin.ShouldLog("Bug3-UV-err", 30f))
                    Plugin.Log.LogError($"[Bug3] TreadUV prefix error: {ex.Message}");
            }
        }

        // Server-side safety net: force AnimateAuthority when occluded
        private static int _forceCount = 0;

        public static void PhysicsUpdate_Postfix(WheeledBase __instance)
        {
            if (!__instance.HasAuthority || !__instance.IsOccluded)
                return;

            _forceCount++;
            for (int i = 0; i < __instance.Wheels.Count; i++)
            {
                try
                {
                    __instance.Wheels[i].AnimateAuthority();
                }
                catch (Exception ex)
                {
                    Plugin.Log.LogError($"[Bug3] AnimateAuthority[{i}] error: {ex.Message}");
                }
            }
        }
    }

    // =========================================================================
    // BUG 5: Picking up / dragging AIMeE launches player into the air
    //
    // Root cause: DragInSlot() creates a ConfigurableJoint between AIMeE and
    // the player, reducing AIMeE's mass to ~10%. But UpdateEachFrame()
    // continues running mode logic (Follow/Roam/etc.) which applies motor
    // torque through the WheelColliders. That force transfers via the joint
    // to the reduced-mass player → player launched upward/sideways.
    //
    // Fix: POSTFIX on UpdateEachFrame that zeroes motor AND brakes when
    // dragged. Using a postfix (not prefix) so base.UpdateEachFrame() and
    // DynamicThing.UpdatePivotJoint() still run — these handle the joint
    // physics that make AIMeE follow the player's sightline.
    //
    // Key: TargetBrakePower must be 0 (not Power!) because WheelCollider
    // brakes create friction that resists the joint force, making AIMeE
    // "float in one spot" instead of following the player's mouse.
    //
    // TryUnstuck and PathToTarget are also blocked when dragged (see Bug2
    // prefixes) to prevent teleportation/hop while held.
    // =========================================================================
    public static class Bug5_DragFlyingFix
    {
        public static void UpdateEachFrame_Postfix(RobotMining __instance)
        {
            if (__instance.Joint != null || __instance.IsChild)
            {
                // Zero BOTH motor and brakes so AIMeE moves freely with the joint
                __instance.TargetMotorPower = 0f;
                __instance.TargetBrakePower = 0f;
                __instance.TargetSteeringAngle = 0f;

                if (Plugin.ShouldLog($"Bug5-Drag-{__instance.GetInstanceID()}", 10f))
                    Plugin.Log.LogInfo($"[Bug5] AIMeE dragged: zeroed motor+brakes (Joint={__instance.Joint != null}, IsChild={__instance.IsChild})");
            }
        }
    }
}

$content = @'
using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.Reflection;
using Assets.Scripts;
using Assets.Scripts.Networking;
using Assets.Scripts.Objects;
using Assets.Scripts.Objects.Electrical;
using Assets.Scripts.Vehicles;
using BepInEx;
using BepInEx.Logging;
using HarmonyLib;
using UnityEngine;

namespace AimeeBugFixes
{
    [BepInPlugin("com.florpydorp.aimeebugfixes", "AIMeE Bug Fixes", "0.2.0")]
    public class Plugin : BaseUnityPlugin
    {
        internal static ManualLogSource Log;
        private Harmony _harmony;

        // Throttled logging helper: returns true if enough time has passed since last log for this key
        private static readonly Dictionary<string, float> _lastLogTimes = new Dictionary<string, float>();
        internal static bool ShouldLog(string key, float intervalSeconds = 10f)
        {
            float now = Time.time;
            if (!_lastLogTimes.TryGetValue(key, out float lastTime) || (now - lastTime) >= intervalSeconds)
            {
                _lastLogTimes[key] = now;
                return true;
            }
            return false;
        }

        void Awake()
        {
            Log = Logger;
            Log.LogInfo("AIMeE Bug Fixes v0.2.0 loading...");
            Log.LogInfo($"  IsBatchMode={GameManager.IsBatchMode}, RunSimulation={GameManager.RunSimulation}");

            _harmony = new Harmony("com.florpydorp.aimeebugfixes");

            int success = 0;
            int fail = 0;

            // Bug 2: Off-state teleport/hop fix
            TryPatch(ref success, ref fail, "Bug2-TryUnstuck",
                AccessTools.Method(typeof(RobotMining), "TryUnstuck"),
                prefix: new HarmonyMethod(typeof(Bug2_OffStateTeleportFix), nameof(Bug2_OffStateTeleportFix.TryUnstuck_Prefix)));

            TryPatch(ref success, ref fail, "Bug2-PathToTarget",
                AccessTools.Method(typeof(RobotMining), "PathToTarget"),
                prefix: new HarmonyMethod(typeof(Bug2_OffStateTeleportFix), nameof(Bug2_OffStateTeleportFix.PathToTarget_Prefix)));

            TryPatch(ref success, ref fail, "Bug2+5-UpdateEachFrame",
                AccessTools.Method(typeof(RobotMining), "UpdateEachFrame"),
                postfix: new HarmonyMethod(typeof(Bug2and5_UpdateEachFramePostfix), nameof(Bug2and5_UpdateEachFramePostfix.Postfix)));

            // Bug 3: Tread/wheel animation sync on multiplayer clients
            TryPatch(ref success, ref fail, "Bug3-TreadUV",
                AccessTools.Method(typeof(WheeledBase), "PhysicsUpdate"),
                prefix: new HarmonyMethod(typeof(Bug3_WheelAnimationSyncFix), nameof(Bug3_WheelAnimationSyncFix.PhysicsUpdate_Prefix)));

            TryPatch(ref success, ref fail, "Bug3-ServerOccluded",
                AccessTools.Method(typeof(WheeledBase), "PhysicsUpdate"),
                postfix: new HarmonyMethod(typeof(Bug3_WheelAnimationSyncFix), nameof(Bug3_WheelAnimationSyncFix.PhysicsUpdate_Postfix)));

            Log.LogInfo($"AIMeE Bug Fixes: {success} patches applied, {fail} failed");
            if (fail > 0)
                Log.LogError("Some patches failed! Check errors above.");
        }

        private void TryPatch(ref int success, ref int fail, string name,
            MethodInfo original, HarmonyMethod prefix = null, HarmonyMethod postfix = null)
        {
            try
            {
                if (original == null)
                {
                    Log.LogError($"  [{name}] FAILED - target method not found via reflection");
                    fail++;
                    return;
                }
                _harmony.Patch(original, prefix: prefix, postfix: postfix);
                Log.LogInfo($"  [{name}] OK - patched {original.DeclaringType.Name}.{original.Name}");
                success++;
            }
            catch (Exception ex)
            {
                Log.LogError($"  [{name}] FAILED - {ex.GetType().Name}: {ex.Message}");
                fail++;
            }
        }

        void OnDestroy()
        {
            Log.LogInfo("AIMeE Bug Fixes: plugin component destroyed (patches remain active)");
        }
    }

    // =========================================================================
    // BUG 2: Off-state hopping/teleporting when receiving logic
    //
    // Root cause: UpdateEachFrame() has no OnOff/Powered guard before the mode
    // switch. When off but an IC10/logic transmitter sets Mode to 1-5:
    //   - TryUnstuck() detects no movement in 60s, calls Unstuck() (teleport)
    //   - PathToTarget() applies AddForce upward (hop)
    //
    // Also blocks TryUnstuck/PathToTarget while being dragged (Bug 5 support).
    // =========================================================================
    public static class Bug2_OffStateTeleportFix
    {
        public static bool TryUnstuck_Prefix(RobotMining __instance)
        {
            // Bug5: Block TryUnstuck when being dragged (would teleport player+AIMeE)
            if (__instance.Joint != null || __instance.IsChild)
            {
                if (Plugin.ShouldLog($"Bug5-Unstuck-{__instance.GetInstanceID()}", 10f))
                    Plugin.Log.LogInfo($"[Bug5] TryUnstuck: BLOCKED (being dragged)");
                return false;
            }
            if (!__instance.OnOff || !__instance.Powered)
            {
                if (Plugin.ShouldLog($"Bug2-Unstuck-{__instance.GetInstanceID()}", 10f))
                    Plugin.Log.LogInfo($"[Bug2] TryUnstuck: BLOCKED (OnOff={__instance.OnOff}, Powered={__instance.Powered})");
                return false;
            }
            return true;
        }

        public static bool PathToTarget_Prefix(RobotMining __instance)
        {
            // Bug5: Block PathToTarget when being dragged
            if (__instance.Joint != null || __instance.IsChild)
            {
                if (Plugin.ShouldLog($"Bug5-Path-{__instance.GetInstanceID()}", 10f))
                    Plugin.Log.LogInfo($"[Bug5] PathToTarget: BLOCKED (being dragged)");
                return false;
            }
            if (!__instance.OnOff || !__instance.Powered)
            {
                if (Plugin.ShouldLog($"Bug2-Path-{__instance.GetInstanceID()}", 10f))
                    Plugin.Log.LogInfo($"[Bug2] PathToTarget: BLOCKED (OnOff={__instance.OnOff}, Powered={__instance.Powered})");
                return false;
            }
            return true;
        }
    }

    // =========================================================================
    // BUG 2 + BUG 5 combined UpdateEachFrame postfix
    //
    // Bug 2: Enforce braking when off (residual motor from mode methods)
    // Bug 5: Zero motor AND brakes when dragged so joint moves AIMeE freely
    //
    // Must be a POSTFIX (not prefix) so base.UpdateEachFrame() runs first.
    // DynamicThing.UpdateEachFrame calls UpdatePivotJoint() which manages
    // the drag joint physics — skipping it would break view-following.
    //
    // Key insight for Bug 5: TargetBrakePower must be 0 (NOT Power) because
    // WheelCollider brakes create friction that resists the joint force,
    // making AIMeE "float in one spot" instead of following the mouse.
    // =========================================================================
    public static class Bug2and5_UpdateEachFramePostfix
    {
        public static void Postfix(RobotMining __instance)
        {
            if (!GameManager.RunSimulation) return;

            // Bug 5: Dragged — zero everything so joint moves AIMeE freely
            if (__instance.Joint != null || __instance.IsChild)
            {
                __instance.TargetMotorPower = 0f;
                __instance.TargetBrakePower = 0f;
                __instance.TargetSteeringAngle = 0f;

                if (Plugin.ShouldLog($"Bug5-Drag-{__instance.GetInstanceID()}", 10f))
                    Plugin.Log.LogInfo($"[Bug5] Dragged: zeroed motor+brakes (Joint={__instance.Joint != null}, IsChild={__instance.IsChild})");
                return;
            }

            // Bug 2: Off — enforce braking
            if (!__instance.OnOff || !__instance.Powered)
            {
                __instance.TargetBrakePower = __instance.Power;
                __instance.TargetMotorPower = 0f;
            }
        }
    }

    // =========================================================================
    // BUG 3: Tread/wheel UV animation broken on multiplayer clients
    //
    // Root cause: WheelColliders disabled on non-authority clients →
    // WheelAudio() can't read rpm → WheelRpm stays 0 → no UV scroll
    //
    // Fix:
    //   A) PREFIX (client): Estimate WheelRpm from synced RigidBody.velocity
    //   B) POSTFIX (server): Force AnimateAuthority when occluded (safety net)
    // =========================================================================
    public static class Bug3_WheelAnimationSyncFix
    {
        private static readonly Dictionary<int, float> _smoothVelocity = new Dictionary<int, float>();
        private const float RpmSmoothTime = 0.1f;

        public static void PhysicsUpdate_Prefix(WheeledBase __instance)
        {
            if (__instance.HasAuthority || __instance.IsOccluded) return;

            try
            {
                Vector3 rbVel = __instance.RigidBody.velocity;
                float velocity = rbVel.magnitude;

                if (velocity > 0.15f)
                {
                    float forwardDot = Vector3.Dot(rbVel.normalized, __instance.ThingTransform.forward);
                    float sign = forwardDot >= 0f ? 1f : -1f;

                    for (int i = 0; i < __instance.Wheels.Count; i++)
                    {
                        var wheel = __instance.Wheels[i];
                        float radius = 0.3f;
                        if (wheel.WheelCollider != null)
                        {
                            float r = wheel.WheelCollider.radius;
                            if (r > 0.01f) radius = r;
                        }
                        float targetRpm = sign * Mathf.Clamp(velocity * 60f / (2f * Mathf.PI * radius), -100f, 100f);

                        int key = wheel.GetHashCode();
                        if (!_smoothVelocity.TryGetValue(key, out float sv)) sv = 0f;
                        wheel.WheelRpm = Mathf.SmoothDamp(wheel.WheelRpm, targetRpm, ref sv, RpmSmoothTime);
                        _smoothVelocity[key] = sv;
                    }
                }
                else
                {
                    for (int i = 0; i < __instance.Wheels.Count; i++)
                    {
                        var wheel = __instance.Wheels[i];
                        int key = wheel.GetHashCode();
                        if (!_smoothVelocity.TryGetValue(key, out float sv)) sv = 0f;
                        wheel.WheelRpm = Mathf.SmoothDamp(wheel.WheelRpm, 0f, ref sv, RpmSmoothTime);
                        _smoothVelocity[key] = sv;

                        if (Mathf.Abs(wheel.WheelRpm) < 1f)
                            wheel.WheelRpm = 0f;
                    }
                }
            }
            catch (Exception ex)
            {
                if (Plugin.ShouldLog("Bug3-UV-err", 30f))
                    Plugin.Log.LogError($"[Bug3] TreadUV prefix error: {ex.Message}");
            }
        }

        private static int _forceCount = 0;

        public static void PhysicsUpdate_Postfix(WheeledBase __instance)
        {
            if (!__instance.HasAuthority || !__instance.IsOccluded)
                return;

            _forceCount++;
            for (int i = 0; i < __instance.Wheels.Count; i++)
            {
                try
                {
                    __instance.Wheels[i].AnimateAuthority();
                }
                catch (Exception ex)
                {
                    Plugin.Log.LogError($"[Bug3] AnimateAuthority[{i}] error: {ex.Message}");
                }
            }
        }
    }
}

Now I see the full picture. The queue logic:
1. If queue empty → `ClearMinableQueue()` + `GetAimeeMinableQueue()` (scan for ore)
2. If STILL empty after scan → `return;` ← this is the Bug 4 early return (no driving)
3. If queue has items → set TargetMinable, try MovingToMineable, try mine, then driving

The Bug 4 postfix was correct in design, but when I rewrote Plugin.cs I removed it. The "no logs" issue is simply because Bug 4 patches were deleted. And "MineablesInQueue always 0" — this is actually important: if AIMeE IS mining things (queue was populated, items were consumed), then between scans the queue is 0.
